summary:
• Iran tensions remain the primary Western Hemisphere supply risk, with ceasefire extensions and naval blockades creating uncertainty around potential disruptions to critical chokepoints despite reduced immediate escalation concerns.

• Druzhba pipeline repairs enabling Russian oil flow resumption and China's aggressive tender activity suggest geopolitical realignment of crude flows that could redirect barrels away from traditional Western Hemisphere markets.

• Production growth constraints across non-OPEC suppliers—including declining Norwegian gas output and slower U.S. inventory builds—limit spare capacity to offset any supply shocks from escalating Iran geopolitical tensions.
